▼

Amazon Nova Act Action Viewer

▼ Prompt
You are performing a browser evidence capture demo. Use only the URLs that are explicitly provided in this prompt. Summarize visible facts only. Do not search for a person by name on LinkedIn or Google. Do not make personal judgments, and do not contact anyone. Only inspect information already visible in the current browser session. Narrate actions briefly while moving. Scroll only when needed. At most 2 scroll passes per section. If you overscroll, use direct navigation, page anchors, or return to the previous visible section instead of endless scrolling. Candidate label for notes: Nguyen Phan Nguyen. GITHUB STAGE: COMPLETE THESE 30 EVIDENCE-CAPTURE STEPS G1. Navigate directly to this GitHub URL: https://github.com/ngstephen1 G2. Wait for the profile page to load. G3. Capture the visible profile URL. G4. Capture the username exactly as shown. G5. Capture the display name exactly as shown. G6. Capture the visible bio or tagline. G7. Capture follower and following counts if visible. G8. Capture visible location, organization, website, or employer links if shown. G9. Capture the visible contributions summary or contribution count if shown. G10. Capture the visible pinned repository names. G11. Capture the primary language and stars for each visible pinned repository if shown. G12. Capture visible topic tags or profile highlights if shown. G13. Open the Repositories tab if it is not already selected and if doing so helps surface visible facts. G14. Capture the visible public repository count if shown. G15. Identify the strongest visible repository to inspect first based on visible title and description. G16. Open that repository. G17. Capture the repository name, owner, and description. G18. Capture the visible language, stars, forks, and last-updated timing if shown. G19. Inspect the top of the README and capture whether setup, demo, or usage information is visible. G20. Capture whether screenshots, demo links, badges, or release markers are visible. G21. Capture whether the visible file tree suggests organized project structure. G22. Capture whether tests, CI config, lint config, notebooks, or build files are visibly present. G23. Return to the main GitHub profile. G24. Open one more visible strong repository if available. G25. Capture its name, description, language, and stars if visible. G26. Capture whether the README looks substantial or minimal. G27. Return to the main profile again. G28. Note the overall visible project themes across the profile such as ML, frontend, systems, data, music tech, or hackathon work. G29. Set GITHUB_STATUS: ok after you have reviewed the provided GitHub URL. G30. End the GitHub stage immediately after the visible profile and up to two visible repositories have been reviewed. FINAL REPORT: F1. After GitHub evidence is captured, call return() immediately for this stage. F2. The final return() must be plain text and include these lines exactly: F3. GITHUB_STATUS: ok/skipped F4. SUMMARY: F5. - 4 to 8 bullets of visible GitHub facts only such as username, bio, followers, contributions, pinned repos, languages, stars, README quality, and repo themes. F6. Do not include LINKEDIN_STATUS or WEB_STATUS in this stage return. F7. Do not include judgments or claims beyond visible page facts.
